title: Glassbox updates and exp support
summary:
Spent the last few days fixing some weird bugs in Glassbox. The log function was using '|' which Python thought was bitwise OR, so it failed. Fixed that with a regex. Also fixed the power clamp; it was stuck at 100, which killed high-degree polynomials. Raised it to 1e8 and now the MSE matches up.

Added support for exp(-x) by adding omega parameters to the exp operator. The evolution engine is also better now. I increased Adam steps and tightened the cleanup tolerances so it doesn't delete good terms by mistake. 

Finally, I added some better logging tools. You can now use --analyze or shrink logs with gzip. It helps keep the file size down when running long evolutions. The C++ backend is now the default.
commitMessages: fix(eval+benchmark): log formula parsing + Power epsilon precision
feat(evolution): Power clamp fix for high-degree polynomials
feat(evolution): evolution engine improvements for pure SR
feat(evolution): add exp(-x) support + tighten cleanup tolerances
feat(logging): optimize evolution pipeline logs and add shrinkage utility
